narrative.context: You notice you’re snapping at small things today. Your shoulders are tense and you feel impatient in conversations.
narrative.prompt: What is the most accurate way to perceive what’s happening emotionally?

options.label: Check body cues and likely triggers
options.description: Notice tension and impatience; scan for triggers (sleep, hunger, stress, unresolved worry) before acting.

ei.rationale: Uses internal cues (body + attention) to perceive emotion accurately and separate triggers from targets.

options.label: Assume everyone else is annoying
options.description: Decide people are the problem and avoid them.

ei.rationale: May reduce conflict short-term, but misses self-cues and true trigger; perception remains incomplete.

options.label: Ignore it until it goes away
options.description: Continue as normal without noticing patterns.

ei.rationale: Fails to perceive emotion signals; likely leads to reactive behavior.

options.label: Label yourself as a 'bad person'
options.description: Assume irritation means something is wrong with you.

ei.rationale: Turns emotion into global self-judgment; blocks accurate perception of triggers and needs.
